steps.title: Spot the contrast that “Consequently” must resolve
steps.content: The passage contrasts tragedies (universal themes, widely accessible to modern audiences) with history plays (require knowledge of centuries of English history). This contrast is what “Consequently” should lead from.

steps.title: State the logical consequence of the contrast
steps.content:
Choice A completes the logic directly: modern audiences find history plays less engaging than tragedies.

Choices B, C, and D either introduce unrelated works, repeat existing information without drawing a consequence, or contradict the established contrast.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
